topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, bright and insistent, right in the center of my screen. My heart gave a sudden, frantic thump against my ribs. It was a direct message from Mr. Harrison. I stared at the profile picture—the same slightly blurred photo he used for his department website—and a strange, warm flush crept up my neck. I hadn't even thought about him in days, just the usual grind of homework and deadlines. Now, seeing his name there, coupled with the casual, almost friendly tone of his message asking about my recent essay, I felt a surge of unexpected confidence. I took a deep breath, smoothed down my shirt, and typed back a thoughtful response, my fingers moving with a steady assurance I didn't know I possessed. It felt like I was stepping onto a stage, ready to face anything.

[story 2]

It was late, the campus library quiet save for the hum of the ventilation system. I was scrolling through my feed, completely absorbed in a thread about astrophysics, when I saw it: a post from Professor Albright. He had tagged me in a photo from a recent lecture, smiling broadly. A slow, internal warmth spread through my chest, banishing the usual anxiety that clung to me like static. This wasn't just a casual acknowledgment; it felt like an opening. I leaned back in my chair, a slow, deliberate smile curving my lips. I didn't immediately reply, instead taking a moment to organize my thoughts, feeling a quiet strength settle in my core. When I finally typed out a comment, it was clear and composed, a testament to a newfound sense of self-possession.

[story 3]

I almost dropped my phone. The screen displayed a direct message from Ms. Evans, my English teacher. She had sent a picture of a book she was reading, and below it, a short note referencing a theme from our recent novel. A wave of something powerful washed over me—a sense of being seen, truly seen, by someone I admired. My hands, usually fidgety, became still on the desk. I felt a quiet resolve harden within me. Instead of retreating into my shell, I pulled up my keyboard. I crafted a reply, ensuring every word conveyed respect and intelligence, a clear demonstration of my own capability. The fear dissolved, replaced by a steady, determined energy.
